artifact: DirectLLM RCA: Rank districts by status-D loan count, compare the top three with the bottom three, and inspect unemployment, crime, and salary features. Report shared feature patterns as correlations only; the requested output is an outlier-versus-control profile, not a causal model.
sql: WITH d_counts AS (SELECT d.district_id, d.A2 AS district, d.A3 AS region, d.A11 AS avg_salary, d.A13 AS unemployment_rate, d.A16 AS crime_rate, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS status_d_loans FROM district d LEFT JOIN account a ON a.district_id=d.district_id LEFT JOIN loan l ON l.account_id=a.account_id AND l.status='D' GROUP BY d.district_id,d.A2,d.A3,d.A11,d.A13,d.A16), ranked AS (SELECT *, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY status_d_loans DESC) AS high_rank, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY status_d_loans ASC) AS low_rank FROM d_counts) SELECT CASE WHEN high_rank <= 3 THEN 'top_3_status_D' WHEN low_rank <= 3 THEN 'bottom_3_status_D' ELSE 'middle' END AS comparison_group, district, region, status_d_loans, avg_salary, unemployment_rate, crime_rate FROM ranked WHERE high_rank <= 3 OR low_rank <= 3 ORDER BY comparison_group, status_d_loans DESC;

charts.title: Top vs Bottom District Feature Profile
filters: top 3 and bottom 3 by status-D count
